title: Understanding Young's Modulus
text: Young's modulus is a measure of the stiffness of a material. It is defined as the ratio of stress to strain. In this problem, you will use Young's modulus to find the change in length of the pencil lead.

title: Formula for Young's Modulus
text: The formula for Young's modulus (E) is: $$E=\frac{stress}{strain}$$. Stress is the force applied per unit area, and strain is the change in length divided by the original length.

title: Calculate Stress
text: Stress is calculated as force divided by area. Use the formula: $$stress=\frac{force}{area}$$. The area of the lead can be found using the formula for the area of a circle: $$area=\pi \left(\frac{diameter}{2}\right)^2$$.

title: Calculate Strain
text: Strain is the change in length divided by the original length. Use the formula: $$strain=change$$ in $$\frac{length}{original}$$ length.

title: Calculate the Area of the Lead
text: Given the diameter of the lead is $$0.50$$ mm, calculate the area using the formula: $$area=\pi \left(\frac{diameter}{2}\right)^2$$.

title: Calculate the Stress
text: Using the force of $$4.0$$ N and the area calculated, find the stress using the formula: $$stress=\frac{force}{area}$$.

title: Calculate the Strain
text: Using Young's modulus (1.0*10**9 N/m**2) and the stress calculated, find the strain using the formula: $$strain=\frac{stress}{Young's}$$ modulus.

title: Calculate the Change in Length
text: Using the original length of $$60$$ mm and the strain calculated, find the change in length using the formula: change in $$length=strain original$$ length.
